text:
A lovely venue at the University of North Carolina at Chapel Hill. My daughter had her undergraduate chemistry graduation here, beautiful “Carolina blue” ceiling. Excellent lighting and acoustics! Feels like a special building for a special occasion.
Also, gorgeous chandeliers on the ceiling. Not over the top, just really elegant.

title: Memorial Hall

name: Noland Taylor
text: Nice venue. There is not a bad seat in the house. Great sound acoustics as it was designed to accommodate musical performances. If you buy tickets for any event at this facility, don't worry about being close or far from the stage, as seats are all close enough to thoroughly enjoy any performance, play, opera, concert, dance, etc. Note, that the reason it gets a 4 star and not a 5 star is due to limited parking near the venue. But this is often the case, unfortunately these days, at many college campuses where parking is typically more prevalent on the perimeter of campus. Even then, you can be dropped off right in front by Uber, taxi, or a driver in your group...so there are convenient ways to deal with many first-world type issues in life. Enjoy the performance!

text: Fantastic acoustics, beautiful theater. Leg room is a little on the small side (at least from where we were sitting on the mezzanine level) so if you have long legs I'd definitely recommend sitting at the edge of a row or right at the balcony. Nonetheless, this is a beautiful venue and I'd highly recommend catching a show here! It's also walking distance to Franklin Street, where are there lots of lovely restaurants and cafes to go to before and/or after a concert :^)

name: Panowork.com魔鬼哥哥
text: Parking is a problem in Memorial Hall, chapel hill NC. You can enjoy opera, ballet, drama at the same time you start to worry about your car is not being ticketed. But don't let that ruin your night. Just think of it as a game. How long can you stay in your seat before you have to run outside and check on your vehicle? Will you make it to the intermission or will you miss the climax? It's a thrilling challenge that adds some spice to your cultural experience. And who knows, maybe you'll get lucky and find a spot right next to the entrance. Or maybe you'll get unlucky and find a boot on your tire. Either way, you'll have a story to tell.
responseFromOwnerText: Thanks for taking the time to share your positive experience. We truly appreciate it!

name: Morad Saad
text: This is a performing arts center with theater and concert halls. The North Carolina Symphony, Opera ,Ballet and others performs there it's a very nice place. We attended many performances there since we moved to Raleigh from new York its similar to Lincoln center on a smaller scale but the performances are great. If you get the chance to attend a performance there do it you will have a great time guaranteed.

name: Craig Smith
text:
Went to see Ben Vereen and the NC symphony with my girlfriend about 8 months ago. Poor signage to the hall where he was to perform in.

Ushers were useless. When we finally found the theater we seated ourselves. Five minutes later an usher appears, only to tell us we are too early to be seated.

Next time we went was for JC Superstar,  since I was now familiar with the venue,  I didn't need assistance from their staff, which made for a pleasent evening.

name: Fritz Gugelmann
text: A delightful concert venue with great variety. Acoustics are much improved since the renovation, and the seats are fairly comfortable. I've sat in most of the sections and found them good. The front row in the balcony, while an excellent view, is short on leg room. I don't know how parking works, but I'm usually able to park at the planetarium.
